description_long: This is a large, traditional wooden barrel with a deep reddish-brown color, reinforced by three wide, weathered metal hoops—one at the top, one at the middle, and one at the bottom. The wood staves are vertically aligned and show visible grain and wear, giving the barrel an aged, rustic appearance. A prominent black skull and crossbones symbol is painted on the side, suggesting hazardous or pirate-themed contents. The metal bands are slightly tarnished and secured with evenly spaced rivets, adding to the sense of durability and age. The top and bottom surfaces are flat and made of the same wood, with the top being fully enclosed and flush with the rim. There are no visible handles or openings, and the barrel appears solid and robust, suitable for storage or decorative use in urban or themed environments.
description: A large, weathered wooden barrel with metal hoops and a black skull and crossbones symbol on its side, giving it a hazardous or pirate-themed look. The barrel is fully enclosed, sturdy, and aged, with no visible handles.
description_view_0: The front view shows the skull and crossbones symbol most clearly, along with the full curvature of the barrel and the weathered metal hoops.
description_view_1: This side view partially reveals the skull and crossbones symbol, with the wood grain and metal bands still prominent.
description_view_2: The symbol is visible but more to the right, and the curvature of the barrel is emphasized, showing the even spacing of the metal hoops.
description_view_3: The skull and crossbones are mostly obscured, highlighting the wood texture and the continuous metal reinforcement.
